item.title: Senate confirms Mullin as homeland security secretary
item.description: Mullin's immediate challenge will be brokering an agreement to end the shutdown, which has furloughed roughly 100,000 DHS employees and disrupted services from TSA airport security to FEMA disaster relief. Senate Democrats have blocked five attempts to pass a DHS funding bill, insisting on enforcement reforms, while Republicans have rejected Democratic proposals to fund non-immigration agencies...

item.title: Iran parliament speaker Ghalibaf ID'd as Trump negotiation partner
item.description: Within hours of Trump's announcement, Ghalibaf posted on X denying any talks had occurred. "No negotiations have been held with the United States," he wrote, calling the reports an attempt "to manipulate financial and oil markets and to escape the quagmire in which the United States and Israel are entangled". Iran's Foreign Ministry echoed the denial, and state broadcaster IRIB called Trump's...

item.title: Colombian military plane crashes after takeoff, killing at least one
item.description:
President Petro seized on the disaster to renew his push for upgrading Colombia's aging military fleet. "I will grant no further delays; it is the lives of our young people that are at stake," he wrote on X. "If civilian or military administrative officials are not up to this challenge, they must be removed."
Colombia first acquired C-130 aircraft in the late 1960s and has since updated some...

item.title: Israel used Iran's own street cameras to track and kill Khamenei
item.description:
The operation has sent alarm through the region. Gulf nations have begun reassessing their own camera networks, and Israel's National Cyber Directorate has warned hundreds of Israeli camera owners to update passwords after Iranian hackers launched retaliatory scanning operations targeting cameras across Israel and the Gulf.
"The infrastructure authoritarian states build to make their rule...

item.title: Judge appoints career prosecutor as NJ's US attorney
item.description:
Unlike earlier appointments, Frazer's selection appears to have the backing of both the judiciary and the administration. Habba, now a senior adviser to Bondi, praised the choice on the social media platform X. "New Jersey deserves a great chief law enforcement officer who is in line with the President's agenda," she wrote, according to the Associated Press.
The resolution comes as courts in...

item.title: Jury awards $19.25M to woman who accused Cosby of 1972 assault
item.description: More than 60 women have accused Cosby of drugging and assaulting them over the course of decades. Motsinger's case was filed in September 2023 under California's Sexual Abuse and Cover Up Accountability Act, which created a look-back window allowing previously time-barred civil claims to proceed. Cosby continues to deny all allegations against him.

item.title: Bahrain seeks UN approval of force to reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The diplomatic push at the United Nations comes after weeks of tension between Washington and its allies over the crisis. President Trump called on roughly seven countries to send warships to escort tankers through the strait, singling out NATO members, China, Japan, Australia, and South Korea. When most declined, he posted on Truth Social that the U.S. "no longer 'need,' or desire, the NATO...

item.title: Pakistan emerges as lead mediator between US and Iran
item.description: Pakistan's involvement carries particular urgency. The country shares a land border with Iran and has the second-largest Shia Muslim population after its neighbor. Officials have warned that Pakistan could run out of liquefied natural gas by April if deliveries remain disrupted. Islamabad also maintains a mutual defense pact with Saudi Arabia signed last year, giving it ties to both sides of the...

item.title: Warren calls Pentagon's Anthropic blacklist 'retaliation'
item.description:
Anthropic filed suit against the Defense Department on March 9, arguing the designation violated its First Amendment rights and due process protections. The company has said the blacklist could slash billions from its 2026 revenue and that the government has been pressuring its customers to switch to rival AI providers.
The case has drawn unusual support from Anthropic's competitors. Microsoft...

item.title: Toyota invests $1B in Kentucky, Indiana plants
item.description: The announcement coincided with a celebration marking 40 years since Toyota broke ground in Georgetown in 1986. The plant has produced more than 14 million vehicles since opening and represents more than $11 billion in total investment. Kentucky Governor Andy Beshear called the partnership transformative. "Toyota Kentucky is Toyota's largest vehicle manufacturing plant in the world," Beshear...

item.title: China urges US, Israel to halt Middle East fighting, warns of 'vicious cycle'
item.description: Trump's original Saturday ultimatum demanded Iran reopen the strait or face the destruction of its power plants. On Monday, he posted on Truth Social that he had instructed "the Department of War to postpone any and all military strikes against Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for a five day period". He later told reporters: "If it goes well, we're going to end up with settling...

item.title: CBS poll: 57% say Iran war not going well as economic fears grow
item.description: An Emerson College poll released March 19 found that 47 percent of likely voters oppose the military action, against 40 percent who support it. The economy has surged to the top of voter concerns, cited by 34 percent as the most important issue — up four points from February. An NPR/PBS News/Marist survey put Trump's approval on the economy at just 35 percent, the lowest of his...

item.title: Russia and Vietnam sign deal to build Vietnam's first nuclear plant
item.description:
The signing comes as Vietnam grapples with soaring fuel costs. Since the U.S.-Israeli military campaign against Iran began in late February, the price of petrol and diesel in Vietnam has climbed by 50 and 70 percent respectively, according to The Straits Times. Chinh has held phone calls with Qatar, Kuwait, Algeria, and Japan seeking fuel support.
Beyond nuclear energy, the two prime ministers...

item.title: Trump admin pays TotalEnergies $928M to exit offshore wind
item.description:
The deal is a blow to New York's offshore wind targets. Attentive Energy was expected to generate enough power for more than one million homes, while Carolina Long Bay could have served roughly 300,000 homes by the early 2030s. New York Governor Kathy Hochul called the prospect of the agreement "not helpful" last week.
The question now is whether this settlement becomes a template for unwinding...

item.title: EU-Mercosur trade deal to take effect provisionally May 1
item.description: France, which led unsuccessful efforts to block the deal over concerns that cheaper South American agricultural goods would undercut its farmers, remains opposed. The accord imposes strict quotas on products such as beef and sugar and includes staggered tariff reduction timelines, while the EU has offered subsidies to help European farmers adjust. The agreement still requires a final European...

item.title: Both pilots killed as jet hits fire truck at LaGuardia
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board has dispatched a "Go Team" to the site and is leading the investigation into the runway incursion and communication breakdown. Investigators will examine cockpit voice and flight data recorders, air traffic control communications, and the sequence of clearances that placed both the aircraft and the fire truck on the same runway simultaneously.

item.title: Trump claims Iran agreed to endnuclear program as Tehran denies any talks
item.description: Trump gave the talks a five-day window. "If it goes well, we'll wrap it up. Otherwise, we'll keep bombing," he said. The war, which began February 28 with joint US-Israeli strikes that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and dozens of senior officials, has claimed more than 1,500 lives in Iran and displaced millions across the region. A CBS News/YouGov poll released Sunday found 57 percent of...

item.title: Wright calls oil disruptions 'temporary,' urges producers to boost output
item.description: The energy picture shifted Monday morning when President Donald Trump announced a five-day pause on planned strikes against Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure, citing "very good and productive" talks with Iranian officials — though Tehran denied any negotiations had taken place, according to Argus Media. Oil prices fell sharply on the news before recovering. Wright, for his part,...

item.title: Newark Airport grounds flights after smoke forces tower evacuation
item.description:
The ground stop at Newark came just hours after a far more serious incident at nearby LaGuardia Airport, where an Air Canada Express regional jet collided with a Port Authority fire truck while landing late Sunday night, killing both pilots. LaGuardia remained closed through at least 2 p.m. Monday, diverting flights to other area airports including Newark.
The simultaneous disruptions at two of...

item.title: Trump rejects bipartisan TSA funding deal, prolonging 37-day shutdown
item.description:
Hours after killing the deal, Trump announced that ICE agents would deploy to airports starting Monday, with border enforcement chief Tom Homan leading the operation. A document obtained by The New York Times indicates ICE personnel will operate at 14 airports, including hubs in New York, Philadelphia, Atlanta, New Orleans, Houston, and Phoenix.
Homan told CNN's "State of the Union" that agents...
